exercises.instruction_pt: Ouça e repita a palavra. O app vai verificar sua pronúncia.
exercises.target_text: Hello
exercises.tip_pt: Diga 'rê-LÔU' — o 'H' em inglês é aspirado, como um sopro leve.

exercises.target_text: Thank you
exercises.tip_pt: O 'TH' é o som mais difícil para brasileiros. Coloque a língua entre os dentes e sopre. Diga 'fênk iú' (aproximado).

exercises.target_text: Good morning
exercises.tip_pt: 'Good' rima com 'pud' (não 'gude'). 'Morning' — o 'R' é diferente do português.

exercises.target_text: Excuse me
I understand
exercises.tip_pt: 'Understand' = ân-der-STÊND. Não adicione vogal depois do 'D' final — NÃO diga 'understandi'.

exercises.instruction_pt: Ouça e repita a frase completa:
exercises.target_text: My name is Maria
exercises.tip_pt: 'My' rima com 'ai'. 'Name' rima com 'heim'. Ligação: 'name-iz' (o 'is' gruda no 'name').

exercises.target_text: I am from Brazil
exercises.tip_pt: 'Brazil' em inglês: brê-ZIL (o 'a' some e o 'Z' é forte). Não diga 'Brasiu'.

exercises.target_text: Nice to meet you
exercises.tip_pt: 'Nice' = NAISS. 'Meet' = MIIT (vogal longa). NÃO diga 'naice tu mitch iu'.

exercises.target_text: I don't understand
exercises.tip_pt: 'Don't' = DÔUNT (não 'dont'). Importante: frase essencial quando não entender algo!

exercises.target_text: Can you speak slowly, please?
exercises.tip_pt: 'Slowly' = SLÔU-li. Use essa frase sempre que alguém falar rápido demais!

exercises.instruction_pt: Ouça e repita a frase completa:
exercises.target_text: I live in São Paulo and I study medicine
exercises.audio_script: I live in São Paulo and I study medicine
exercises.phonetic_hint: /aɪ lɪv ɪn saʊ ˈpaʊloʊ ænd aɪ ˈstʌdi ˈmɛdəsən/
accepted_variations: i live in sao paulo and i study medicine
i live in são paulo and i study medicine

exercises.target_text: I want to study abroad next year
exercises.audio_script: I want to study abroad next year
exercises.phonetic_hint: /aɪ wɒnt tə ˈstʌdi əˈbrɔːd nɛkst jɪr/
accepted_variations: i want to study abroad next year

exercises.target_text: I need to practice my English every day
exercises.audio_script: I need to practice my English every day
exercises.phonetic_hint: /aɪ niːd tə ˈpræktɪs maɪ ˈɪŋɡlɪʃ ˈɛvri deɪ/
accepted_variations: i need to practice my english every day

exercises.target_text: The weather is beautiful today, isn't it?
exercises.audio_script: The weather is beautiful today, isn't it?
exercises.phonetic_hint: /ðə ˈwɛðər ɪz ˈbjuːtɪfəl təˈdeɪ ˈɪzənt ɪt/
accepted_variations: the weather is beautiful today isn't it

exercises.target_text: Could you tell me where the nearest hospital is?
exercises.audio_script: Could you tell me where the nearest hospital is?
exercises.phonetic_hint: /kʊd juː tɛl miː wɛr ðə ˈnɪrəst ˈhɒspɪtəl ɪz/
exercises.tip_pt: 'Could you' na fala rápida vira 'cud-ju'. 'Nearest' = NIIR-est (mais perto).
accepted_variations: could you tell me where the nearest hospital is

exercises.instruction_pt: Ouça a pergunta e responda em voz alta. Siga o modelo sugerido.
exercises.question_audio: What is your name?
exercises.target_text: My name is [name]
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'My name is' + seu nome

exercises.question_audio: Where are you from?
exercises.target_text: I am from Brazil
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I am from Brazil' ou 'I'm from [sua cidade]'

exercises.question_audio: What do you do?
exercises.target_text: I am a student
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I am a student' ou 'I study medicine'

exercises.question_audio: How old are you?
exercises.target_text: I am twenty-five years old
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I am [número] years old'

exercises.question_audio: What time do you wake up?
exercises.target_text: I wake up at seven o'clock
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I wake up at [hora] o'clock'

exercises.instruction_pt: Descreva o que você vê usando as palavras sugeridas. Fale em voz alta.
exercises.scene_description_pt: Uma família sentada à mesa jantando.
exercises.target_text: There is a family sitting at the table. They are eating dinner. They look happy.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'There is a family...' 'They are eating...' 'They look...'

exercises.scene_description_pt: Um homem correndo no parque de manhã cedo.
exercises.target_text: There is a man running in the park. It is early in the morning. He is exercising.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'There is a man...' 'It is early...' 'He is...'

exercises.scene_description_pt: Estudantes numa sala de aula, o professor escrevendo no quadro.
exercises.target_text: There are students in a classroom. The teacher is writing on the board.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'There are students...' 'The teacher is...'

exercises.scene_description_pt: Uma mulher comprando frutas no mercado.
exercises.target_text: There is a woman at the market. She is buying fresh fruits.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'There is a woman...' 'She is buying...'

exercises.scene_description_pt: Chuva forte, pessoas com guarda-chuvas na rua.
exercises.target_text: It is raining heavily. People are walking on the street with umbrellas. The street is wet.
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'It is raining...' 'People are...' 'The street is...'

exercises.instruction_pt: Ouça a pergunta e responda em voz alta. Fale pelo menos 2 frases.
exercises.question_audio: What time do you usually wake up?
exercises.target_text: I usually wake up at six thirty in the morning
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I usually wake up at...' + adicione um detalhe (ex: 'I set two alarms')

exercises.question_audio: How do you get to school or work?
exercises.target_text: I take the bus to university
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I take the bus/car/train to...' + quanto tempo demora

exercises.question_audio: What do you usually have for lunch?
exercises.target_text: I usually have rice beans and some kind of meat for lunch
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I usually have...' + onde você almoça

exercises.question_audio: What do you do after school or work?
exercises.target_text: After university I usually go to the library and study
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'After [school/work], I usually...' + atividade

exercises.question_audio: What time do you usually go to bed?
exercises.target_text: I usually go to bed around eleven PM
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I usually go to bed around...' + o que faz antes de dormir

exercises.instruction_pt: Ouça a pergunta e dê sua opinião. Use 'I think... because...'
exercises.question_audio: Do you prefer studying alone or in a group?
exercises.target_text: I prefer studying alone because I can concentrate better
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I prefer... because...' + dê um exemplo

exercises.question_audio: Do you think social media is good or bad for students?
exercises.target_text: I think social media can be both good and bad
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I think... because...' + dê um lado positivo e um negativo

exercises.question_audio: Would you prefer to live in a big city or a small town?
exercises.target_text: I would prefer to live in a big city because there are more opportunities
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I would prefer... because...'

exercises.question_audio: Do you think learning English online is effective?
exercises.target_text: Yes I think learning English online can be very effective
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'Yes/No, I think... because...'

exercises.question_audio: What is more important money or free time?
exercises.target_text: I think free time is more important than money
exercises.hint_pt: Diga: 'I think [X] is more important because...'

exercises.instruction_pt: Descreva o processo passo a passo. Use: First... Then... After that... Finally...
exercises.question_audio: How do you make coffee?
exercises.target_text: First I boil some water then I put coffee in the filter after that I pour the water finally I add sugar
exercises.hint_pt: Use conectores: First → Then → After that → Finally

exercises.question_audio: How do you prepare for an exam?
exercises.target_text: First I organize my notes then I review the important topics after that I do practice questions finally I get a good night sleep
exercises.hint_pt: Use: First → Then → After that → Finally

exercises.question_audio: Describe the process of registering for classes at your university.
exercises.target_text: First you log into the student portal then you search for the courses after that you add each course finally you click submit
exercises.hint_pt: Use: First → Then → After that → Finally

exercises.question_audio: How do you get from your home to your university?
exercises.target_text: First I walk to the bus stop then I take the bus after I get off I walk to my building
exercises.hint_pt: Descreva cada etapa do trajeto

exercises.question_audio: Describe what you do when you arrive at the university.
exercises.target_text: When I arrive I first go to the cafeteria then I check my schedule after that I head to class
exercises.hint_pt: Descreva sua rotina ao chegar

exercises.instruction_pt: Compare as duas opções. Use: 'X is more/less... than Y', 'On the other hand...'
exercises.question_audio: Compare living in a big city and living in a small town.
exercises.target_text: Living in a big city is more convenient because there are more stores and restaurants on the other hand small towns are quieter
exercises.hint_pt: Use: '...is more [adj] than...' + 'On the other hand...' + 'However...'

exercises.question_audio: Compare studying at a university and studying online.
exercises.target_text: Studying at a university gives you face-to-face interaction however online studying is more flexible
exercises.hint_pt: Dê uma vantagem de cada um

exercises.question_audio: Compare traveling by plane and traveling by car.
exercises.target_text: Traveling by plane is much faster however traveling by car gives you more freedom
exercises.hint_pt: Use: '...is faster/cheaper/more comfortable than...'

exercises.question_audio: Compare reading a book and watching a movie.
exercises.target_text: Reading a book allows you to use your imagination more movies are more visual and entertaining
exercises.hint_pt: Dê uma vantagem de cada opção

exercises.question_audio: Compare working in an office and working from home.
exercises.target_text: Working in an office allows for better collaboration working from home saves commuting time
exercises.hint_pt: Mencione vantagens e desvantagens de cada um

exercises.instruction_pt: Conte uma experiência passada. Use passado simples (I went, I saw, I ate).
exercises.question_audio: Tell me about a trip you took recently.
exercises.target_text: Last year I took a trip to the south of Brazil I went with my family we visited many beautiful places
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'Last [month/year] I went to...' + o que você fez + como foi

exercises.question_audio: Describe a challenge you faced and how you overcame it.
exercises.target_text: One of the biggest challenges I faced was when I failed my first exam I changed my study method and got a better grade
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'One challenge I faced was...' + 'I overcame it by...'

exercises.question_audio: Tell me about a time you helped someone.
exercises.target_text: Last month I helped an elderly woman who was lost I walked with her to the bus stop
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'Last [time], I helped...' + o que aconteceu + como você ajudou

exercises.question_audio: Describe your best day at university.
exercises.target_text: My best day at university was when I presented my research project my professor complimented my work
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'My best day was when...' + o que aconteceu + por que foi especial

exercises.question_audio: Tell me about something new you learned recently.
exercises.target_text: Recently I learned how to use Python for data analysis at first it was confusing but after practice I got better
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'Recently I learned...' + como foi no começo + como melhorou

exercises.instruction_pt: Responda explicando razões. Use 'because', 'since', 'as a result'.
exercises.question_audio: Why is learning English important for your career?
exercises.target_text: Learning English is important for my career because most scientific research is published in English and international companies require English proficiency
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'Learning English is important because...' + dê 2-3 razões

exercises.question_audio: What happens when students don't get enough sleep?
exercises.target_text: When students don't get enough sleep they have difficulty concentrating in class as a result their grades may decrease
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'When students don't get enough sleep...' + 'as a result...'

exercises.question_audio: Why do many young Brazilians want to study abroad?
exercises.target_text: Many young Brazilians want to study abroad because they can get better education improve their language skills and have international experience
exercises.hint_pt: Use: '...because...' + liste razões com 'and'

exercises.question_audio: What are the effects of climate change?
exercises.target_text: Climate change causes rising sea levels more extreme weather events and loss of biodiversity these changes affect millions of people worldwide
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'Climate change causes...' + liste efeitos

exercises.question_audio: Why is exercise important for mental health?
exercises.target_text: Exercise is important for mental health because it releases endorphins which reduce stress and anxiety regular physical activity also improves sleep quality and self-esteem
exercises.hint_pt: Use: '...because...' + 'also...'

exercises.instruction_pt: Dê conselhos. Use: 'I would recommend', 'You should', 'It's a good idea to'.
exercises.question_audio: A friend wants to improve their English. What advice would you give?
exercises.target_text: I would recommend practicing English every day even if it is just for thirty minutes you should watch movies in English with subtitles and try to read articles online
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'I would recommend...' + 'You should...' + 'It's also a good idea to...'

exercises.question_audio: What should a student do to prepare for the TOEFL?
exercises.target_text: First you should take a practice test to know your current level then focus on your weakest skills I would also recommend doing timed practice to get used to the test format
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'First you should...' + 'Then...' + 'I would also recommend...'

exercises.question_audio: A colleague is feeling very stressed at work. What would you suggest?
exercises.target_text: I would suggest taking short breaks during the day you should also try to exercise regularly and make sure you get enough sleep it is important to maintain a work-life balance
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'I would suggest...' + 'You should also...' + 'It is important to...'

exercises.question_audio: What advice would you give to someone moving to a new country?
exercises.target_text: I would advise learning some basic phrases in the local language before you arrive you should also research the culture and customs to avoid misunderstandings try to make local friends as soon as possible
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'I would advise...' + 'You should also...' + 'Try to...'

exercises.question_audio: How can someone save money while studying at university?
exercises.target_text: To save money at university you should cook at home instead of eating out you can also buy used textbooks and look for student discounts sharing an apartment with roommates is another good way to reduce costs
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'You should...' + 'You can also...' + '...is another good way to...'

theme: Narrar eventos no passado com detalhes

exercises.instruction_pt: Conte um evento passado com detalhes. Use past tense + descrições.
exercises.question_audio: Describe a memorable class you had at school or university.
exercises.target_text: One of my most memorable classes was a biology lecture about the human brain the professor brought a real brain to class and let us examine it I was fascinated by how complex the brain is
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'One of my most memorable... was...' + descreva o que aconteceu + como você se sentiu

exercises.question_audio: Tell me about a time you had to speak English in a real situation.
exercises.target_text: Last year I had to speak English when I met a tourist who was lost near my university he asked me for directions to the bus station I was nervous at first but I managed to help him and he thanked me
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'Last [time]...' + o que aconteceu + como você se sentiu + resultado

exercises.question_audio: Describe a time you worked on a group project. How did it go?
exercises.target_text: In my third semester we had to do a group project about public health there were four people in my group at first it was difficult because we had different ideas but we learned to compromise and in the end we got a good grade
exercises.hint_pt: Descreva: o projeto + as dificuldades + como resolveram + resultado

exercises.question_audio: Describe a time you failed at something and what you learned from it.
exercises.target_text: When I was in high school I failed my first math exam I had not studied enough and I was overconfident after that experience I learned to take my studies more seriously I started creating a study schedule and my grades improved significantly
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'When I was...' + o que aconteceu + o que aprendeu + como mudou

exercises.question_audio: Describe your first day at university.
exercises.target_text: My first day at university was both exciting and overwhelming I remember feeling nervous as I walked into the campus for the first time the buildings were much bigger than my school I got lost trying to find my classroom but a senior student helped me eventually I found my class and met some of my classmates who I am still friends with today
exercises.hint_pt: Descreva: como se sentiu + o que viu + o que aconteceu + resultado

exercises.instruction_pt: Argumente a favor OU contra. Use: 'I believe...', 'One argument is...', 'However...', 'In conclusion...'.
exercises.question_audio: Do you think university education should be free for everyone?
exercises.target_text: I believe university education should be free because many talented students cannot afford tuition and education is a fundamental right however I understand that funding would need to come from taxes in conclusion I think the benefits outweigh the costs
exercises.hint_pt: Estrutura: opinião → razão 1 → contra-argumento com 'however' → conclusão

exercises.question_audio: Should animals be used for scientific research?
exercises.target_text: This is a difficult question on one hand animal research has led to important medical breakthroughs that save human lives on the other hand animals suffer during experiments and we should respect their welfare I think we should invest more in alternative methods while gradually reducing animal testing
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'On one hand...' + 'On the other hand...' + conclusão equilibrada

exercises.question_audio: Is social media doing more harm than good to society?
exercises.target_text: In my opinion social media is doing more harm than good firstly it contributes to mental health problems especially among young people secondly it spreads misinformation rapidly although social media helps people stay connected I believe the negative effects are greater
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'In my opinion...' + 'Firstly...' + 'Secondly...' + 'Although...' + conclusão

exercises.question_audio: Should all students learn to code?
exercises.target_text: I think all students should learn basic coding skills because technology is part of every career today understanding how software works helps people think logically and solve problems even if they don't become programmers these skills are valuable in any profession
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião + razões + contra-argumento + conclusão

exercises.question_audio: Is it better to specialize in one field or to have knowledge in many areas?
exercises.target_text: I believe it is better to have deep knowledge in one field while also having some understanding of other areas specialization allows you to become an expert but having broad knowledge helps you see connections between different disciplines the best professionals combine both depth and breadth
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'I believe...' + vantagens de cada lado + conclusão equilibrada

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o texto curto, depois resuma em voz alta em 45 segundos. Use: 'According to the text...', 'The main point is...'.
exercises.question_audio: Summarize this: Studies show that students who eat breakfast perform better academically than those who skip it. Breakfast provides the brain with glucose, which is essential for concentration and memory. Schools that offer free breakfast programs have seen improvements in attendance and test scores.
exercises.target_text: According to the text eating breakfast helps students perform better academically breakfast provides glucose which the brain needs for concentration schools with free breakfast programs have seen better attendance and test scores
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'According to the text...' + resuma os 2-3 pontos principais

exercises.question_audio: Summarize this: The number of people working remotely has tripled since 2020. Companies have found that remote workers are often more productive, save money on office space, and report higher job satisfaction. However, some managers worry about communication challenges and maintaining team culture.
exercises.target_text: The text says that remote work has tripled since 2020 companies found that remote workers are more productive and happier however there are concerns about communication and team culture
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'The text says that...' + pontos principais + 'however...'

exercises.question_audio: Summarize this: Deforestation in the Amazon decreased by 30% in 2023 compared to the previous year, according to Brazil's space agency INPE. Experts attribute this decline to stronger enforcement of environmental laws and increased satellite monitoring. However, illegal logging remains a significant challenge, particularly in remote areas.
exercises.target_text: According to the passage Amazon deforestation decreased by thirty percent in 2023 this was due to stronger law enforcement and better satellite monitoring however illegal logging is still a problem especially in remote areas
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'According to the passage...' + causa + 'however...'

exercises.question_audio: Summarize this: A new study published in Nature Medicine found that a Mediterranean diet supplemented with extra virgin olive oil reduced the risk of cardiovascular disease by 30% compared to a control diet. The study followed 7,447 participants over five years. Researchers concluded that dietary changes can be as effective as medication for preventing heart disease.
exercises.target_text: The study found that a Mediterranean diet with olive oil reduced heart disease risk by thirty percent over five years with more than seven thousand participants researchers concluded that diet can be as effective as medication for preventing heart disease
exercises.hint_pt: Use: dados + conclusão principal

exercises.question_audio: Summarize this: The World Health Organization reports that depression is the leading cause of disability worldwide, affecting more than 280 million people. Despite effective treatments being available, more than 75% of people in low and middle-income countries receive no treatment. Barriers include lack of resources, shortage of trained healthcare providers, and social stigma associated with mental illness.
exercises.target_text: According to the WHO depression affects over 280 million people worldwide and is the leading cause of disability however more than 75 percent of people in developing countries don't receive treatment because of lack of resources few trained providers and social stigma
exercises.hint_pt: Use: 'According to the WHO...' + dados + barreiras

theme: TOEFL Speaking Task 1 — Opinião pessoal (45 segundos)

exercises.instruction_pt: Dê sua opinião em 45 segundos. Estrutura: Opinião → Razão 1 + exemplo → Razão 2 + exemplo → Conclusão.
exercises.question_audio: Some people prefer to live in a big city. Others prefer to live in a small town. Which do you prefer and why?
exercises.target_text: I prefer living in a big city for two main reasons first big cities offer more career opportunities especially in fields like medicine and technology for example São Paulo has hundreds of hospitals and research centers second cities have better infrastructure including public transportation museums and universities although cities are more expensive and stressful I believe the opportunities they provide are worth it
exercises.hint_pt: 45 seg: Opinião (5s) → Razão 1 + exemplo (15s) → Razão 2 + exemplo (15s) → Conclusão (10s)

exercises.question_audio: Do you agree or disagree with the following statement? Students should be required to take physical education classes in university.
exercises.target_text: I agree that university students should take physical education classes first regular exercise has been proven to reduce stress and improve academic performance which is especially important during exam periods second many students develop sedentary habits in university that can lead to health problems later in life requiring PE classes would help establish healthy routines early
exercises.hint_pt: Agree/Disagree → Razão 1 + evidência → Razão 2 + evidência → Conclusão

exercises.question_audio: Some people believe that it is better to study alone. Others prefer studying in groups. Which do you think is more effective?
exercises.target_text: I believe studying alone is generally more effective for two reasons first when you study alone you can focus completely without distractions and move at your own pace second you develop stronger self discipline and independent thinking skills however I think group study can be useful for reviewing before exams because explaining concepts to others helps you understand them better
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião → 2 razões → Concessão com 'however'

exercises.question_audio: Do you think it is important for young people to learn a second language? Why or why not?
exercises.target_text: I strongly believe that learning a second language is essential for young people first it opens doors to international career opportunities and higher salaries research shows that bilingual professionals earn up to 20 percent more second learning another language improves cognitive abilities like memory problem solving and multitasking in my own experience studying English has allowed me to access scientific research that is not available in Portuguese
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião forte → Razão 1 + dados → Razão 2 + benefícios cognitivos → Exemplo pessoal

exercises.question_audio: Some people think that technology has made our lives easier. Others believe it has made life more complicated. What is your opinion?
exercises.target_text: In my opinion technology has made our lives easier overall first it has revolutionized communication allowing us to connect with anyone anywhere in the world instantly second technology has made information more accessible through the internet we can learn almost anything for free however I acknowledge that technology has also created new problems like addiction to social media and cybersecurity threats on balance though I believe the benefits far outweigh the drawbacks
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião → 2 benefícios → Concessão → Conclusão balanceada

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o anúncio, depois explique a mudança e dê sua opinião em 60 segundos.
exercises.question_audio: University announcement: Starting next semester, the library will close at 10 PM instead of midnight. The administration says this will reduce operating costs and improve campus safety. Do you agree with this change?
exercises.target_text: The university has decided to close the library at 10 PM instead of midnight to reduce costs and improve safety I disagree with this decision for two reasons first many students especially those preparing for exams rely on the library for late night studying closing earlier will force them to find alternative places which may be less suitable second the argument about safety is weak because the library is one of the safest places on campus I think the university should find other ways to cut costs without affecting students study habits
exercises.hint_pt: Resuma a mudança (15s) → Sua opinião + 2 razões (45s)

exercises.question_audio: University policy: All first-year students must live in campus dormitories. The university says this helps students adjust to college life and build a sense of community. Do you think this is a good policy?
exercises.target_text: The university requires all first year students to live on campus to help them adjust and build community I think this is a good policy for the most part first living on campus makes it easier for new students to make friends and participate in university activities second it eliminates the stress of finding housing in an unfamiliar city however I think exceptions should be made for students who live with their families nearby or who have financial constraints that make dorm life unaffordable
exercises.hint_pt: Resuma a política → Opinião + razões → Exceções/nuance

exercises.question_audio: Professor's email: Dear students, I have decided to replace the final exam with a group research project. Each group of 4 students will choose a topic, conduct research, and present their findings to the class. What do you think of this change?
exercises.target_text: The professor has replaced the final exam with a group research project I have mixed feelings about this change on one hand research projects develop important skills like teamwork critical thinking and presentation abilities that exams don't test on the other hand group projects can be problematic because some members may not contribute equally which is unfair to hardworking students I would prefer if the professor offered a choice between the exam and the project so that students could select the format that best suits their learning style
exercises.hint_pt: Resuma a mudança → Prós → Contras → Sugestão alternativa

theme: TOEFL Speaking Task 3 — Leitura + Áudio + Resumo

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o conceito acadêmico, depois explique usando o exemplo em 60 segundos.
exercises.question_audio: Academic concept: 'Confirmation bias' is the tendency to search for, interpret, and remember information that confirms our pre-existing beliefs while ignoring contradictory evidence. Example: A student who believes that studying with music helps concentration might only notice the times they study well with music, while ignoring the many times they were distracted by it.
exercises.target_text: Confirmation bias is when people tend to favor information that supports what they already believe the reading explains that we search for and remember evidence that confirms our beliefs while ignoring evidence that contradicts them the professor gives a clear example of a student who thinks music helps them study this student remembers the times music helped but forgets the times it was distracting this shows how confirmation bias can lead us to make inaccurate conclusions about what actually works
exercises.hint_pt: Defina o conceito (15s) → Explique com o exemplo (30s) → Conclusão (15s)

exercises.instruction_pt: Explique o conceito com o exemplo em 60 segundos:
exercises.question_audio: Academic concept: 'The bystander effect' occurs when individuals are less likely to offer help to a victim when other people are present. The more bystanders there are, the less likely anyone is to help, because each person assumes someone else will take action. Example: In 1964, Kitty Genovese was attacked in New York while reportedly 38 witnesses watched from their apartments without calling the police.
exercises.target_text: The bystander effect is a psychological phenomenon where people are less likely to help someone in need when others are present each person assumes that someone else will take action the reading describes the famous case of Kitty Genovese in 1964 who was attacked in New York while many witnesses reportedly watched without helping this tragic example illustrates how the presence of other bystanders reduces individual feelings of responsibility the more people present the less likely any single person is to act
exercises.hint_pt: Defina (15s) → Exemplo (30s) → Por que acontece (15s)

theme: TOEFL Speaking Task 4 — Resumir lecture acadêmica

exercises.instruction_pt: Ouça a mini-lecture e resuma os pontos principais em 60 segundos.
exercises.question_audio: Lecture summary: The professor discusses two strategies companies use to compete in the market. The first is cost leadership, where a company offers the lowest prices by reducing production costs — like Walmart, which uses its massive buying power to negotiate lower prices from suppliers. The second is differentiation, where a company makes its product unique — like Apple, which charges premium prices because its products are perceived as innovative and high-quality. The professor notes that trying to do both simultaneously usually fails.
exercises.target_text: The professor discusses two competitive strategies first is cost leadership where companies compete by offering the lowest prices the example is Walmart which uses its buying power to get lower prices from suppliers second is differentiation where companies make their products unique and charge premium prices the example is Apple which is perceived as innovative the professor emphasizes that companies should choose one strategy because trying to do both at the same time usually leads to failure
exercises.hint_pt: Estratégia 1 + exemplo (20s) → Estratégia 2 + exemplo (20s) → Ponto principal (20s)

exercises.question_audio: Lecture summary: The professor explains two types of memory. Short-term memory, also called working memory, holds information for about 15-30 seconds and has a capacity of about 7 items. This is why phone numbers have 7 digits. Long-term memory has virtually unlimited capacity and can store information for a lifetime. The process of transferring information from short-term to long-term memory is called 'encoding,' and it is enhanced by repetition, emotional connections, and relating new information to things you already know.
exercises.target_text: The professor explains two types of memory short term memory holds information for about 15 to 30 seconds and can store about 7 items which is why phone numbers have 7 digits long term memory has unlimited capacity and can last a lifetime the process of moving information from short term to long term is called encoding the professor says encoding is improved through repetition emotional connections and relating new information to existing knowledge
exercises.hint_pt: Memória curta prazo (20s) → Memória longa prazo (15s) → Encoding e como melhorar (25s)

exercises.instruction_pt: SIMULADO — Task 1 (45 segundos): Dê sua opinião.
exercises.question_audio: Do you agree or disagree? It is more important for students to understand ideas and concepts than to learn facts.
exercises.target_text: I agree that understanding concepts is more important than memorizing facts for two reasons first concepts provide a framework for understanding new information while facts without context are quickly forgotten for example understanding how the immune system works is more valuable than memorizing the names of every white blood cell second in today's world facts are easily accessible online but the ability to analyze evaluate and apply concepts requires deep understanding in conclusion education should prioritize conceptual understanding over rote memorization
exercises.hint_pt: Opinião → Razão 1 + exemplo → Razão 2 → Conclusão

exercises.instruction_pt: SIMULADO — Task 2 (60 segundos): Leia o anúncio e dê sua opinião.
exercises.question_audio: University announcement: The cafeteria will now offer only healthy food options. Fried foods, sugary drinks, and processed snacks have been removed from the menu. The university says this will improve student health and reduce healthcare costs. A student disagrees, saying that adults should have the freedom to choose what they eat.
exercises.target_text: The university has decided to remove unhealthy food from the cafeteria menu I partially agree with the student who disagrees while I understand the university's goal of improving health I believe adults should have the freedom to make their own choices a better approach would be to offer both healthy and unhealthy options but make the healthy choices more visible affordable and attractive this way the university promotes health without restricting personal freedom research shows that making healthy options the default choice is more effective than banning unhealthy ones
exercises.hint_pt: Resuma → Posição nuançada → Solução alternativa → Evidência

exercises.instruction_pt: SIMULADO — Task 3 (60 segundos): Explique o conceito acadêmico.
exercises.question_audio: Concept: 'Anchoring bias' is when people rely too heavily on the first piece of information they receive when making decisions. Example: In a salary negotiation, whoever states a number first sets the 'anchor.' If a company offers $50,000 first, the negotiation tends to stay near that figure, even if the candidate deserves $70,000. Studies show that even random numbers can influence decisions — judges who rolled dice before sentencing gave longer sentences when the dice showed higher numbers.
exercises.target_text: Anchoring bias is a cognitive bias where the first piece of information we receive heavily influences our decisions the professor gives two examples first in salary negotiations whoever states a number first sets an anchor if a company offers 50000 the discussion stays near that number even if the candidate deserves much more second even random numbers affect decisions judges who rolled dice before sentencing gave longer sentences when dice showed higher numbers this demonstrates that anchoring works subconsciously and can lead to irrational decisions
exercises.hint_pt: Defina (10s) → Exemplo 1 (20s) → Exemplo 2 (20s) → Conclusão (10s)

exercises.instruction_pt: SIMULADO — Task 4 (60 segundos): Resuma os pontos da lecture.
exercises.question_audio: Lecture: The professor discusses two theories of language acquisition. Noam Chomsky's nativist theory proposes that humans are born with an innate language acquisition device (LAD) — a biological capacity for learning language. Evidence includes the fact that all children follow similar stages of language development regardless of culture. The behaviorist theory, associated with B.F. Skinner, argues that language is learned through imitation, reinforcement, and practice. Evidence includes the fact that children raised in language-rich environments develop larger vocabularies. The professor concludes that modern linguists believe both nature and nurture play important roles.
exercises.target_text: The professor discusses two theories of language acquisition first Chomsky's nativist theory says humans are born with an innate capacity for language called the language acquisition device evidence for this is that all children develop language in similar stages regardless of their culture second Skinner's behaviorist theory argues that language is learned through imitation and reinforcement children in language rich environments develop larger vocabularies which supports this view the professor concludes that modern linguists believe both theories are partially correct language acquisition involves both innate biological capacity and environmental input
exercises.hint_pt: Teoria 1 + evidência (20s) → Teoria 2 + evidência (20s) → Conclusão do professor (20s)

exercises.instruction_pt: Explique o conceito acadêmico em 60 segundos como se estivesse ensinando a um colega.
exercises.question_audio: Explain the concept of 'supply and demand' and give an example.
exercises.target_text: Supply and demand is a fundamental economic concept the law of demand says that when the price of a product goes up fewer people buy it and when the price goes down more people buy it supply works the opposite way producers are willing to make more of something when the price is high for example during a heat wave the demand for air conditioners increases but if the supply stays the same prices go up
exercises.hint_pt: Defina (15s) → Explique demanda (15s) → Explique oferta (15s) → Dê exemplo (15s)

exercises.question_audio: Explain what 'natural selection' means and how it works.
exercises.target_text: Natural selection is the process by which organisms that are better adapted to their environment are more likely to survive and reproduce this idea was proposed by Charles Darwin for example imagine a population of beetles some are green and some are brown birds eat the green beetles more easily because they stand out against brown soil over time there are more brown beetles because they survive and pass on their genes this is natural selection in action
exercises.hint_pt: Defina → Quem propôs → Dê exemplo concreto → Conclusão

exercises.question_audio: What is inflation and how does it affect people's lives?
exercises.target_text: Inflation is when the general price level of goods and services increases over time which means your money buys less than it used to for example if inflation is 10 percent something that cost 100 reais last year now costs 110 reais inflation affects everyone especially people on fixed incomes like retirees because their income stays the same while prices go up central banks try to control inflation by adjusting interest rates
exercises.hint_pt: Defina → Dê exemplo com números → Quem é mais afetado → Como é controlado

exercises.question_audio: What is the greenhouse effect and why is it important?
exercises.target_text: The greenhouse effect is a natural process where certain gases in the atmosphere trap heat from the sun keeping the earth warm enough to support life without it the earth would be about 33 degrees colder the problem is that human activities like burning fossil fuels are increasing the concentration of these greenhouse gases especially carbon dioxide this is causing the earth to warm more than normal leading to climate change rising sea levels and extreme weather events
exercises.hint_pt: Defina → Por que é importante naturalmente → Qual é o problema → Consequências

exercises.question_audio: Explain what 'cognitive dissonance' means and give an example.
exercises.target_text: Cognitive dissonance is the mental discomfort you feel when you hold two contradictory beliefs or when your actions contradict your beliefs for example a person who knows that smoking is unhealthy but continues to smoke experiences cognitive dissonance to reduce this discomfort people often change their beliefs rather than their behavior the smoker might tell themselves that the health risks are exaggerated this concept was developed by psychologist Leon Festinger in 1957
exercises.hint_pt: Defina → Dê exemplo → Explique como pessoas resolvem → Quem criou o conceito

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Sintetizar informações de duas fontes

exercises.instruction_pt: Leia o texto e a opinião do professor. Explique como a lecture complementa ou contradiz o texto em 60 segundos.
exercises.question_audio: Text: Homework reinforces learning and improves test scores. Lecture: The professor says excessive homework causes stress and that Finland achieves top education results with minimal homework. Explain how the lecture relates to the reading.
exercises.target_text: The reading argues that homework reinforces learning and improves test scores however the professor presents a different perspective she points out that excessive homework can cause stress and negatively affect student well-being she uses Finland as an example a country that assigns very little homework yet consistently achieves top scores in international education rankings this contradicts the reading's assumption that more homework leads to better academic outcomes the professor suggests that the quality of classroom instruction matters more than the quantity of homework assigned
exercises.hint_pt: Resuma o texto (15s) → O que o professor diz (20s) → Como se relacionam (15s) → Conclusão (10s)

exercises.instruction_pt: Sintetize as duas fontes em 60 segundos:
exercises.question_audio: Text: Standardized testing is the fairest way to evaluate students because everyone takes the same test. Lecture: The professor argues that standardized tests are biased against students from low-income backgrounds because they can't afford test prep courses, and that the tests measure test-taking ability rather than actual knowledge.
exercises.target_text: The reading claims that standardized testing is fair because all students take the same exam under the same conditions however the professor challenges this view by arguing that standardized tests are actually biased against students from low-income backgrounds wealthy students can afford expensive preparation courses which gives them an unfair advantage furthermore the professor argues that these tests measure test-taking skills rather than genuine understanding of the material this suggests that while the tests appear equal on the surface they actually reinforce existing inequalities
exercises.hint_pt: O texto diz X → O professor argumenta Y → Evidência → O que isso sugere

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Apresentar soluções para problemas

exercises.instruction_pt: Descreva o problema e proponha 2 soluções com justificativa em 60 segundos.
exercises.question_audio: Many university students experience high levels of stress and anxiety. What solutions would you propose?
exercises.target_text: Student stress and anxiety have become serious problems at universities I would propose two solutions first universities should provide free counseling services and mental health support accessible to all students many students don't seek help because they can't afford private therapy or don't know where to find resources on campus second universities should consider reducing course loads and creating more flexible deadlines the pressure to take many courses simultaneously combined with strict deadlines creates an environment where stress becomes unavoidable these changes would not lower academic standards but would create a healthier learning environment
exercises.hint_pt: Problema (10s) → Solução 1 + justificativa (20s) → Solução 2 + justificativa (20s) → Conclusão (10s)

exercises.question_audio: Air pollution in big cities is a growing health crisis. What two measures would you recommend to address this problem?
exercises.target_text: Air pollution in major cities is causing serious health problems including respiratory diseases and premature deaths I would recommend two key measures first governments should invest heavily in public transportation systems including subways buses and bike lanes to reduce the number of private cars on the road cities like Copenhagen and Amsterdam have shown that good cycling infrastructure can dramatically reduce car usage second factories and power plants should be required to meet strict emission standards with heavy fines for violations transitioning to renewable energy sources like solar and wind would address the root cause of industrial pollution
exercises.hint_pt: Problema + impacto (10s) → Medida 1 + exemplo (20s) → Medida 2 + como implementar (20s) → Conclusão (10s)

exercises.instruction_pt: O professor pergunta sua opinião sobre um debate acadêmico. Apresente AMBOS os lados antes de dar sua posição em 60 segundos.
exercises.question_audio: Some researchers argue that intelligence is primarily genetic, while others believe environment plays a bigger role. What is your position?
exercises.target_text: This is one of the most debated topics in psychology on one side researchers point to twin studies which show that identical twins raised apart still have remarkably similar IQ scores suggesting a strong genetic component on the other side environmental factors like quality of education nutrition and early childhood stimulation have been shown to significantly affect cognitive development I believe the answer lies somewhere in the middle genetics may set a range of potential but environment determines where within that range a person actually falls this is sometimes called the nature versus nurture interaction
exercises.hint_pt: Lado 1 + evidência (15s) → Lado 2 + evidência (15s) → Sua posição nuançada (20s) → Conclusão (10s)

exercises.instruction_pt: Apresente ambos os lados e sua posição em 60 segundos:
exercises.question_audio: Should developing countries prioritize economic growth or environmental protection?
exercises.target_text: This is a challenging dilemma for developing countries on one hand economic growth is essential for reducing poverty improving healthcare and building infrastructure millions of people in developing nations lack basic necessities and economic development can provide these on the other hand environmental degradation caused by unchecked industrialization can have devastating long-term consequences including pollution deforestation and climate change which disproportionately affects the poorest populations in my view these goals are not mutually exclusive developing countries can pursue sustainable development that balances economic growth with environmental protection examples like Costa Rica show that it is possible to grow economically while protecting natural resources
exercises.hint_pt: Lado 1 (crescimento) + razões → Lado 2 (meio ambiente) + razões → Sua posição → Exemplo

theme: Simulado TOEFL Speaking — Tasks completas cronometradas

exercises.question_audio: Do you agree or disagree with the following statement? 'Success is primarily the result of hard work rather than talent.'
exercises.target_text: I agree that hard work is more important than talent for achieving success while natural talent gives people a head start it is ultimately effort and persistence that determine long term outcomes for example many talented musicians never succeed because they don't practice consistently while many average musicians become professionals through years of dedicated practice research by psychologist Angela Duckworth supports this showing that grit defined as passion and perseverance is a stronger predictor of success than IQ or talent in conclusion talent may open doors but only hard work keeps them open
exercises.hint_pt: 45 seg. Opinião → Razão + exemplo → Evidência acadêmica → Conclusão forte

exercises.instruction_pt: SIMULADO Task 4 (60 seg): Resuma a lecture acadêmica.
exercises.question_audio: Lecture summary: The professor discusses two types of extinction. Background extinction is the normal, ongoing loss of species due to natural causes like disease, competition, and environmental change — typically 1-5 species per year. Mass extinction is a dramatic, rapid event where 75%+ of species disappear in a geologically short period. There have been five mass extinctions in Earth's history. The most famous, 66 million years ago, killed the dinosaurs. Many scientists argue we are now in a sixth mass extinction, driven not by asteroids but by human activities — habitat destruction, pollution, climate change, and overexploitation. Current extinction rates are estimated to be 100-1,000 times higher than background rates.
exercises.target_text: The professor explains two types of extinction background extinction is the normal ongoing loss of about one to five species per year due to natural causes like disease and competition mass extinction is a catastrophic event where more than 75 percent of species disappear rapidly there have been five mass extinctions in earth history the most famous killed the dinosaurs 66 million years ago the professor argues that we are now experiencing a sixth mass extinction but this one is caused by human activities including habitat destruction pollution and climate change current extinction rates are estimated at 100 to 1000 times higher than normal this suggests that human impact on biodiversity is unprecedented in earth history
exercises.hint_pt: 60 seg. Background extinction (15s) → Mass extinction (15s) → Sixth extinction (20s) → Conclusão (10s)

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Analisar causas e consequências complexas

exercises.instruction_pt: Analise causas e consequências em 60 segundos. Use linguagem acadêmica.
exercises.question_audio: What are the main causes and consequences of the global decline in biodiversity?
exercises.target_text: The decline in biodiversity is driven by several interconnected factors the primary cause is habitat destruction through deforestation and urbanization which eliminates the ecosystems that species depend on climate change compounds this by altering temperature and precipitation patterns faster than many species can adapt additionally pollution overexploitation of resources and invasive species further reduce populations the consequences are far reaching loss of biodiversity weakens ecosystem services such as pollination water purification and carbon sequestration which humans depend on ultimately biodiversity loss threatens food security medicine and the stability of ecosystems that support all life on earth
exercises.hint_pt: Causas (habitat, clima, poluição) → Consequências (ecossistemas, comida, medicina) → Conclusão

exercises.question_audio: Why is income inequality increasing in many countries, and what are its effects on society?
exercises.target_text: Income inequality has been rising due to several factors including globalization which benefits skilled workers while displacing low-skilled jobs technological automation replacing routine tasks tax policies that favor the wealthy and declining labor union membership the effects on society are significant high inequality correlates with worse health outcomes lower social mobility higher crime rates and political polarization research by economists like Thomas Piketty shows that when wealth concentrates at the top economic growth actually slows because lower income people have less purchasing power governments can address this through progressive taxation investment in education and strengthening social safety nets
exercises.hint_pt: Causas (globalização, automação, políticas) → Efeitos (saúde, mobilidade, crime) → Soluções

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Avaliar argumentos e evidências

exercises.instruction_pt: Avalie a força do argumento em 60 segundos. O argumento é forte ou fraco? Por quê?
exercises.question_audio: Evaluate this argument: 'My grandfather smoked for 60 years and lived to 95, so smoking must not be that dangerous.'
exercises.target_text: This is a very weak argument because it relies on a single anecdotal example to contradict extensive scientific evidence the fact that one person smoked and lived long does not disprove the overwhelming statistical data showing that smoking causes cancer heart disease and premature death this is a logical fallacy known as the anecdotal fallacy where personal experience is used to dismiss systematic evidence it is similar to saying seatbelts are unnecessary because someone survived a car crash without one individual exceptions do not invalidate population level trends scientific conclusions are based on studies of millions of people not individual cases
exercises.hint_pt: Identifique a falácia → Explique por que é fraco → Dê analogia → Conclusão sobre evidência científica

exercises.question_audio: A study found that countries with more ice cream sales have higher crime rates. Does this mean ice cream causes crime?
exercises.target_text: No this does not mean ice cream causes crime this is a classic example of the correlation versus causation fallacy just because two things are correlated does not mean one causes the other in this case there is a confounding variable temperature during hot summer months both ice cream sales and crime rates increase independently hot weather drives people to buy ice cream and also leads to more outdoor activity which creates more opportunities for crime the hidden variable temperature explains both trends this is why scientists always say correlation does not imply causation and why controlled experiments are necessary to establish causal relationships
exercises.hint_pt: Correlação ≠ Causação → Variável confundidora (temperatura) → Lição sobre método científico

exercises.instruction_pt: Faça uma mini-apresentação acadêmica de 90 segundos sobre o tema. Estrutura: Introdução → 2-3 pontos → Conclusão.
exercises.question_audio: Give a brief academic presentation on the advantages and disadvantages of telemedicine.
exercises.target_text: Good morning today I would like to discuss the advantages and disadvantages of telemedicine which has grown dramatically since the COVID-19 pandemic first let me address the benefits telemedicine increases access to healthcare especially for patients in rural or underserved areas who may live hours from the nearest specialist it also reduces costs for both patients and providers by eliminating travel time and reducing overhead additionally patients with chronic conditions can receive more frequent monitoring without leaving their homes however telemedicine has important limitations physical examinations are impossible through a screen which means certain diagnoses may be missed elderly patients and those in areas with poor internet connectivity may be excluded from these services there are also concerns about data privacy and the quality of the doctor patient relationship in a virtual setting in conclusion telemedicine is a valuable complement to traditional healthcare but it cannot fully replace in person medical care the ideal approach combines both modalities based on the specific needs of each patient
exercises.hint_pt: 90 seg: Intro (10s) → Vantagens com detalhes (30s) → Desvantagens com detalhes (30s) → Conclusão (20s)

exercises.question_audio: Present the arguments for and against nuclear energy as a solution to climate change.
exercises.target_text: Today I will examine whether nuclear energy should be considered a solution to climate change there are strong arguments on both sides proponents argue that nuclear power produces virtually zero greenhouse gas emissions during operation making it one of the cleanest energy sources available a single nuclear plant can generate enormous amounts of electricity reliably regardless of weather conditions unlike solar and wind which are intermittent france for example generates about 70 percent of its electricity from nuclear power and has one of the lowest carbon footprints in Europe however opponents raise valid concerns nuclear accidents like Chernobyl and Fukushima demonstrated the catastrophic potential of nuclear disasters the problem of radioactive waste storage remains unsolved as waste remains dangerous for thousands of years additionally nuclear plants are extremely expensive and time consuming to build often taking over a decade in my assessment nuclear energy should be part of a diverse energy portfolio but not the sole solution combining nuclear with renewable sources and energy efficiency measures offers the most realistic path to decarbonization
exercises.hint_pt: Intro → Argumentos a favor + exemplo (France) → Argumentos contra + exemplos → Avaliação pessoal

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Responder a perguntas inesperadas

exercises.instruction_pt: Responda a esta pergunta inesperada com organização clara em 45 segundos. Não se perca — use a estrutura: posição → razão → exemplo → conclusão.
exercises.question_audio: If you could change one thing about the education system in your country, what would it be?
exercises.target_text: If I could change one thing about education in Brazil it would be to start teaching English from the first grade currently most public schools only begin English instruction in middle school by which time students have already passed the critical period for language acquisition countries like the Netherlands and Sweden start English education at age six and achieve near native proficiency by adulthood implementing early English education would require training more qualified teachers and developing appropriate curricula but the long term benefits for Brazilian students competitiveness in the global job market would far outweigh the investment
exercises.hint_pt: Escolha UMA coisa → Por que é importante → Exemplo/comparação → Desafio + benefício

exercises.question_audio: What is the most important scientific discovery of the last 100 years and why?
exercises.target_text: I believe the most important scientific discovery of the last century is antibiotics specifically the discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 before antibiotics simple infections like pneumonia and infected wounds were often fatal average life expectancy was around 47 years antibiotics have saved an estimated 200 million lives and enabled modern surgery organ transplants and cancer treatment which would be impossible without the ability to prevent bacterial infections however we now face the crisis of antibiotic resistance which threatens to undo these gains this makes it not only the most important discovery but also one of the most urgent ongoing challenges in medicine
exercises.hint_pt: Escolha → Por que → Impacto com dados → Desafio atual → Por que é o mais importante

theme: Simulado TOEFL Speaking — Prova completa cronometrada

exercises.question_audio: Some universities now offer entire degree programs online. Do you think online degrees are as valuable as traditional degrees? Why or why not?
exercises.target_text: I believe online degrees are becoming increasingly valuable but are not yet fully equivalent to traditional degrees in most fields online programs offer flexibility affordability and access to world class education regardless of location for fields like computer science business and education online degrees have gained significant acceptance among employers however for fields requiring hands-on training such as medicine engineering and laboratory sciences traditional programs remain essential because they provide practical experience that cannot be replicated online additionally the networking opportunities and social development that come from campus life are difficult to reproduce virtually as technology improves and more prestigious universities offer online options the gap between online and traditional degrees will likely continue to narrow
exercises.hint_pt: 45 seg. Posição nuançada → Campo onde funciona → Campo onde não funciona → Tendência futura

exercises.instruction_pt: SIMULADO Task 2 (60 seg): Resumir e opinar.
exercises.question_audio: University policy: The university will replace all paper textbooks with digital versions to reduce costs and environmental impact. Student opinion: A student argues that digital textbooks cause eye strain, are harder to annotate, and that many students prefer physical books for deep reading. What is your opinion?
exercises.target_text: The university plans to switch to digital textbooks to save money and reduce environmental impact while I understand the student's concerns about eye strain and reading preferences I think the switch is ultimately a positive change digital textbooks are significantly cheaper saving students hundreds of dollars per semester and they reduce paper consumption however the university should address the student's valid concerns by providing digital annotation tools training sessions on effective e-reading strategies and ensuring all digital materials are compatible with e-ink readers which reduce eye strain a gradual transition rather than an immediate switch would also help students adapt I believe that with proper implementation digital textbooks can provide the same learning outcomes at lower cost and environmental impact
exercises.hint_pt: Resuma política → Reconheça preocupações → Sua posição → Soluções práticas → Conclusão

exercises.question_audio: Academic concept: 'Moral hazard' occurs when a party takes greater risks because they know someone else will bear the cost of those risks. Example: After the 2008 financial crisis, critics argued that bailing out large banks created moral hazard — if banks know the government will rescue them when they fail, they have less incentive to manage risk responsibly. This is sometimes called 'too big to fail.' Explain this concept using the example.
exercises.target_text: Moral hazard is an economic concept that occurs when someone takes greater risks because they won't bear the full consequences of those risks the professor uses the 2008 financial crisis as an example when large banks made extremely risky investments and nearly collapsed the government bailed them out using taxpayer money critics argue this created moral hazard because if banks know the government will rescue them they have little incentive to be responsible with risk in the future this is called too big to fail the banks benefit from the profits of risky behavior but the public bears the losses when things go wrong this concept extends beyond banking it applies whenever insurance or guarantees reduce the incentive to act carefully for example people with comprehensive car insurance might drive less carefully because they know the insurance company will cover damages
exercises.hint_pt: Defina (10s) → Exemplo bancário (25s) → Extensão do conceito (15s) → Exemplo adicional (10s)

exercises.question_audio: Lecture: The professor discusses the 'tragedy of the commons' using two examples. First, international fishing: because oceans belong to no single nation, fishing fleets from many countries overfish, depleting stocks — over one-third of global fish are now overfished. Second, antibiotic resistance: when individual patients demand antibiotics for viral infections and doctors prescribe them unnecessarily, each interaction seems harmless, but collectively it creates antibiotic-resistant superbugs that threaten everyone. The professor concludes that both examples show how individually rational behavior leads to collectively harmful outcomes when shared resources are involved.
exercises.target_text: The professor explains the tragedy of the commons using two powerful examples first she discusses international fishing since oceans are shared resources that no single country owns fishing fleets from many nations compete to catch as much as possible this has led to severe overfishing with over one third of global fish stocks now depleted second she examines antibiotic resistance when individual patients demand antibiotics for viral infections and doctors prescribe them each case seems harmless but collectively this creates resistant superbugs that threaten public health worldwide the professor's main point is that both examples demonstrate the same fundamental problem individually rational behavior leads to collectively harmful outcomes when shared resources are involved this happens because each person benefits from exploiting the resource while the costs are distributed across everyone
exercises.hint_pt: Exemplo 1 pesca (20s) → Exemplo 2 antibióticos (20s) → Ponto principal + conexão (20s)

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Analisar pesquisas e estudos

exercises.instruction_pt: Analise o estudo em 60 segundos: descreva a metodologia, resultados e limitações.
exercises.question_audio: A study of 500 university students found that those who exercised at least 3 times per week had GPAs 0.4 points higher than those who didn't exercise. Analyze this study.
exercises.target_text: This study found a positive correlation between exercise and academic performance among 500 university students those who exercised three or more times weekly had GPAs 0.4 points higher however there are important limitations to consider first correlation does not imply causation it's possible that students with better time management skills both exercise more and study more effectively second the sample of 500 from a single university may not be representative of all students third the study doesn't mention whether it controlled for factors like socioeconomic status sleep habits or course difficulty despite these limitations the findings are consistent with broader research linking physical activity to improved cognitive function
exercises.hint_pt: Resultados (15s) → Limitação 1: correlação ≠ causação (15s) → Limitação 2-3 (15s) → Contextualização (15s)

exercises.question_audio: A survey found that 78% of employers say they value communication skills more than technical skills when hiring. Discuss the implications of this finding.
exercises.target_text: This survey reveals that the vast majority of employers prioritize communication over technical skills which has several important implications for education and career preparation first it suggests that universities should place greater emphasis on developing students presentation writing and interpersonal skills rather than focusing exclusively on technical knowledge second it implies that students who invest time in activities like debate clubs public speaking and collaborative projects may have a competitive advantage in the job market however we should interpret this data cautiously the survey measures what employers say they value which may differ from what they actually reward in hiring decisions also communication and technical skills are not mutually exclusive the ideal candidate possesses both
exercises.hint_pt: Resultado principal → Implicação 1 (educação) → Implicação 2 (carreira) → Cautela na interpretação

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Integrar múltiplas fontes de informação

exercises.instruction_pt: Integre informação de 3 fontes em 90 segundos: texto, dados e opinião.
exercises.question_audio: Text: 'Universal healthcare reduces inequality and improves population health.' Data: Countries with universal healthcare spend 10-12% of GDP on health, vs 17% in the US. The US has lower life expectancy than all other developed nations despite spending more. Expert opinion: Some economists argue that universal healthcare reduces innovation because pharmaceutical companies have less incentive to develop new drugs. Synthesize these three sources.
exercises.target_text: The text argues that universal healthcare reduces inequality and improves health outcomes the data strongly supports this claim countries with universal systems spend significantly less as a percentage of GDP between 10 and 12 percent compared to 17 percent in the United States yet achieve better health outcomes the US has lower life expectancy than all other developed nations despite being the highest spender this suggests that the US system is both more expensive and less effective however the expert opinion introduces a valid counterpoint some economists warn that universal healthcare could reduce pharmaceutical innovation because companies would have less profit incentive to develop new drugs this is a legitimate concern as the US does lead the world in new drug development nevertheless the data suggests that this innovation premium comes at a very high cost in both money and lives a balanced approach might combine universal coverage with targeted incentives for pharmaceutical research
exercises.hint_pt: Fonte 1 (texto) → Fonte 2 (dados que apoiam) → Fonte 3 (contra-argumento) → Síntese/conclusão

exercises.question_audio: Text: 'Online education democratizes access to learning.' Data: Completion rates for MOOCs (massive open online courses) average 5-15%, compared to 80-90% for traditional courses. Students from wealthy countries are 10x more likely to complete MOOCs than those from developing countries. Expert: A professor argues that online education works best as a supplement to, not replacement for, traditional instruction. Synthesize.
exercises.target_text: The text presents an optimistic view that online education democratizes learning by making it accessible to anyone with an internet connection however the data complicates this narrative significantly MOOC completion rates of only 5 to 15 percent compared to 80 to 90 percent for traditional courses suggest that access alone does not ensure learning more troublingly students from wealthy countries are ten times more likely to complete online courses than those from developing countries this means that rather than reducing educational inequality online education may actually be reinforcing it the expert opinion offers a more nuanced perspective suggesting that online education works best as a supplement to traditional instruction rather than a replacement this hybrid approach could potentially combine the accessibility of online learning with the structure and support that traditional education provides addressing the completion rate problem while maintaining the democratizing potential of digital education
exercises.hint_pt: Texto (otimista) → Dados (complicam) → Opinião especialista (nuance) → Sua síntese

theme: TOEFL Speaking — Responder a situações éticas

exercises.instruction_pt: Discuta a questão ética em 60 segundos com argumentos dos dois lados:
exercises.question_audio: Is it ethical for pharmaceutical companies to charge high prices for life-saving medications?
exercises.target_text: This is a complex ethical question on one hand pharmaceutical companies argue that high prices are necessary to fund research and development new drugs can cost billions of dollars and take over a decade to develop without the possibility of profit companies would have less incentive to invest in creating new treatments on the other hand charging unaffordable prices for life saving medications means that people die from treatable conditions simply because they cannot pay this seems fundamentally unjust especially when taxpayer funded research often contributes to drug development I believe a balanced solution is needed perhaps through government negotiation of drug prices combined with patent reforms that maintain innovation incentives while ensuring access
exercises.hint_pt: Lado 1 (empresas: R&D caro) → Lado 2 (vidas em jogo) → Solução equilibrada

exercises.question_audio: Should genetic testing of embryos be allowed for selecting desired traits like intelligence or physical appearance?
exercises.target_text: Genetic testing of embryos raises profound ethical concerns while screening for serious genetic diseases like cystic fibrosis or sickle cell anemia is widely accepted selecting for traits like intelligence height or eye color crosses into ethically dangerous territory this practice called designer babies could create a genetic divide between those who can afford enhancement and those who cannot potentially creating a new form of inequality furthermore our understanding of genetics is still limited and selecting for one trait could have unpredictable consequences on others I believe genetic testing should be permitted for preventing serious diseases but regulated to prevent selection of non medical traits this draws a clear line between therapy and enhancement
exercises.hint_pt: Doenças (aceitável) vs traços estéticos (problemático) → Riscos → Onde traçar a linha

exercises.instruction_pt: Compare duas teorias em 60 segundos explicando semelhanças, diferenças e qual você acha mais convincente:
exercises.question_audio: Compare Chomsky's nativist theory of language acquisition with Skinner's behaviorist theory. Which do you find more convincing?
exercises.target_text: Chomsky and Skinner proposed fundamentally different explanations for how children learn language Chomsky's nativist theory argues that humans are born with an innate language acquisition device a biological capacity for learning language evidence includes the fact that all children follow similar developmental stages regardless of culture and that children produce sentences they have never heard before suggesting internal rules rather than mere imitation Skinner's behaviorist theory in contrast argues that language is learned through environmental reinforcement children imitate adults and are rewarded for correct usage evidence includes the fact that children in language rich environments develop larger vocabularies I find Chomsky's theory more convincing because it better explains how children master complex grammar so quickly and why they produce creative errors like goed instead of went which imitation alone cannot explain however I believe both nature and nurture contribute the truth likely lies in an interaction between innate capacity and environmental input
exercises.hint_pt: Teoria 1 + evidência → Teoria 2 + evidência → Qual é mais convincente + por quê → Nuance

exercises.question_audio: Compare the 'disease model' and the 'choice model' of addiction. What are the implications of each?
exercises.target_text: The disease model views addiction as a chronic brain disorder supported by brain imaging showing that addiction physically alters neural reward circuits this model reduces stigma encourages people to seek treatment and is endorsed by most medical organizations the choice model argues that addiction involves voluntary behavior and that framing it as a disease removes personal responsibility proponents point to people who quit addictions through willpower alone as evidence the practical implications differ significantly the disease model supports medical treatment insurance coverage and compassionate policies while the choice model tends to favor punishment and personal accountability I believe the disease model is more scientifically supported but acknowledge that the choice model captures something important about human agency the best approach likely combines medical treatment with strategies that empower individuals to make better choices
exercises.hint_pt: Modelo 1 + evidência + implicações → Modelo 2 + evidência + implicações → Sua posição nuançada

exercises.question_audio: Do you agree or disagree: The most important factor in choosing a career is the salary it offers.
exercises.target_text: I disagree that salary is the most important factor in choosing a career while financial security is certainly important research consistently shows that job satisfaction depends more on factors like meaningful work good relationships with colleagues autonomy and opportunities for growth a study by the Harvard Business Review found that employees who find their work meaningful are three times more likely to stay at their jobs than those motivated primarily by salary furthermore careers that offer high salaries but poor work life balance often lead to burnout and health problems which ultimately diminishes the value of the income in my experience the happiest professionals I know chose careers aligned with their passions and values not just the highest paying option
exercises.hint_pt: 45 seg: Disagree → Razão + estudo → Consequência de focar só em salário → Experiência pessoal

exercises.question_audio: Concept: 'Opportunity cost' is the value of the next best alternative that you give up when making a decision. Example: A student who spends 4 years in medical school gives up the salary they could have earned working during those years. The professor also gives the example of a government that spends $10 billion on defense — the opportunity cost is the schools and hospitals that could have been built. Explain the concept using the examples.
